entries.note: V64-A reuses the shipped V59 continuity, V60 continuation, and V61 governed communication lineage by default.
V64-A widens one writable surface only and preserves the shipped local_write/create_new mutation subset.
Continuity region and continuity admission remain persistence/context law, not writable entitlement.
Governed communication lineage may contextualize repo write posture but is not the writable lease.
Selected surface membership and target membership remain canonical, normalized, repo-contained, and fail closed on symlink or alias surprises.
The lease alone is not blanket entitlement; V64-A still requires exact target occupancy/admissibility inside the selected surface.
All-repo authority, shell or execute authority, dispatch widening, and delegated worker authority remain deferred beyond V64-A.
